title: fahb
text: Many trials fail recruit number participants need answer research question definitively. generally want identify trials, call infeasible, early can stopped valuable resources used elsewhere. often specifying first stage study pilot phase, pilot trial, looking recruitment data pilot finished deciding continue remainder trial. Making stop go decision based early recruitment data straightforward. need consider just number participants recruited, also quickly sites opening much variability site recruitment rates.

title: fahb
text: fahb package use hierarchical Poisson mixed model recruitment, means site j∈{1,…,m}j \\{1 , \ldots , m\} recruits participants individual rate γj\gamma_j, rates follow log-normal distribution: lnγj∼N(β,σ2). \ln \gamma_j \sim N(\beta, \sigma^2).  also assume sites open according Poisson process, rate λ\lambda. three parameters model (λ,β,(\lambda, \beta, σ)\sigma) unknown, place prior distributions . Formally, λ∼Gamma(δ,ϵ),β∼N(μ,ν2),σ∼Gamma(ρ,π), \begin{align}     \lambda & \sim Gamma(\delta, \epsilon) ,\\     \beta & \sim N(\mu, \nu^2),\\     \sigma & \sim Gamma(\rho, \pi), \end{align}  δ,ϵ,μ,ν,ρ\delta, \epsilon, \mu, \nu, \rho π\pi known hyperparameters. assume three parameters independent, joint prior product three component priors. model therefore fully specified six hyperparameters.

title: fahb
text: trial internal pilot, trial design specified total number recruit full study, NN, number sites, mm, timing interim analysis, tt (years). final piece necessary information threshold use denote whether trial feasible . specify multiple expected time fully recruit. trial therefore fully specified four variables.

title: fahb
text: Let’s apply framework specifying particular problem. GUSTO trial aimed recruit total N=320N = 320 participants m=20m = 20 sites period 3 years, included internal pilot t=0.5t = 0.5 years recruitment. suppose trial recruits within rel_thr = 1.2 times expected timeframe, consider feasible; otherwise, consider infeasible ideally like stop early. Pre-trial feasibility assessments recruiting sites led estimated total recruitment rate 13 participants per month sites, estimated site opening rate 10.53 sites per year. constructed prior distributions β,σ\beta, \sigma λ\lambda matched expectations allowed realistic degree prior uncertainty. can use information specify problem form fahb_problem object: tried use meaningful names function arguments, splitting hyperparameters three 2-element vectors: so_hps site opening rate λ\lambda (.e. δ\delta ϵ\epsilon); mean_rr_hps central parameter recruitment rate distribution β\beta (.e. μ\mu ν\nu); sd_rr_hps corresponding dispersion parameter σ\sigma (.e. ρ\rho π\pi). generally helpful visualise prior densities check align prior beliefs, fahb help via check_priors() function. addition densities three substantive parameters λ,β\lambda, \beta σ\sigma, also plot implied prior distribution recruitment rate randomly selected site.
code: problem <- fahb_problem(N = 320, m = 20, t = 0.5, rel_thr = 1.2,                          so_hps = c(30, 2.85),                          mean_rr_hps = c(2, 0.329),                          sd_rr_hps = c(30, 100)) plots <- check_priors(problem)  (plots[[1]] + plots[[2]]) / (plots[[3]] + plots[[4]])

what: Operating characteristics to inform design decisions
title: fahb
text: Internal pilots often accompanied pre-specified thresholds three measures recruitment: total number recruited, number sites opened, recruitment rate (per site, per year). thresholds guide progression decision, suggesting keep going three exceeded. thresholds can hard choose, fahb can help us searching thresholds produce good operating characteristics. propose use false positive rate (FPR) false negative rate (FNR); probability progressing (stopping) given trial truly infeasible (feasible). estimate simulating n_sims trials comparing true feasibility (dictated rel_thr) progression decision made decision rule. alternative form decision rule based Bayesian analysis pilot data resulting expectation posterior predictive distribution time recruit. rule simpler sense one threshold specify. expected recruitment time exceeds stop, otherwise can continue. help us choose thresholds can use forecast() function run necessary simulations store fahb_problem object, create fahb_design object takes problem argument: plot shows best operating characteristics possible types decision rules. can look specific OCs attained corresponding thresholds decision rules printing fahb_design object: example, suppose wanted FPR 0.2. two different decision rules minimise FNR constraint : , standard progression criteria tell us proceed recruit least 9 participants least 1 site, overall rate least 4.76. get similar OCs instead run Bayesian analysis proceed expected time recruit 3.15 years. Note many suggested progression criteria negative components always exceeded , therefore, redundant. want improve OCs, need information internal pilot. example, instead wait t=1t = 1 year recruitment period:
code: set.seed(9278635)  # Run the simulations problem <- forecast(problem)  # Find some candidate decision rules are their OCs design <- fahb_design(problem)  plot(design) design # Standard progression criteria: design$Prog_Crit_OCs[design$Prog_Crit_OCs$FPR == 0.2,]  # Approximate Bayesian decision rule: design$Bayes_OCs[design$Bayes_OCs == 0.2,] problem <- fahb_problem(N = 320, m = 20 , t = 1, rel_thr = 1.2,                          so_hps = c(30, 2.85),                          mean_rr_hps = c(2, 0.329),                          sd_rr_hps = c(30, 100))  # Run the simulations problem <- forecast(problem)  # Find some candidate decision rules are their OCs design <- fahb_design(problem)  plot(design) design$Prog_Crit_OCs[design$Prog_Crit_OCs$FPR == 0.2,]

title: fahb
text: collected pilot data, need analyse make progression decision. pre-specified standard progression criteria, trivial - just calculate three summary measures check exceeded chosen thresholds. making decisions using Bayesian approach, analysis involved. need take recruitment data use estimate posterior distribution three parameters model (λ,β\lambda, \beta σ\sigma) random effects sites opened pilot. need use posteriors estimate prior predictive distribution time full recruitment, TT. Suppose pilot data recruitment numbers 4,8,04, 8, 0 22, recruited sites open 0.5,0.4,0.30.5, 0.4, 0.3 0.20.2 years respectively. can use print() plot() methods summarise results, although note posterior predictive samples brmsfit model held within analysis object. allow user summarise results ways please.
code: n_pilot <- c(4, 8, 0, 2) t_pilot <- c(0.5, 0.4, 0.3, 0.2)  analysis <- fahb_analysis(n_pilot, t_pilot, problem) print(analysis)  plots <- plot(analysis) (plots[[1]] + plots[[2]]) / (plots[[3]] + plots[[4]])

title: fahb
text: default fahb assumes internal pilot, can use external pilots . example, suppose use model GUSTO example. Now assume external pilot run three sites either 60 participants recruited reach time t_int = 0.33 expected recruitment time main trial. analysis:
code: problem <- fahb_problem(n_ext = 80, m_ext = 6, t_int = 0.33)  problem <- forecast(problem)  design <- fahb_design(problem)  plot(design) design n_pilot <- c(4, 8, 0, 2) t_pilot <- c(0.5, 0.4, 0.3, 0.2) site_t <- 0.6  analysis <- fahb_analysis(n_pilot, t_pilot, problem, site_t)  print(analysis)  plots <- plot(analysis) (plots[[1]] + plots[[2]]) / (plots[[3]] + plots[[4]])

what: Design and Analysis of Pilot Trials Assessing Recruitment Feasibility
title: Design and Analysis of Pilot Trials Assessing Recruitment Feasibility
text: goal fahb help clinical trial feasibility assessment via hierarchical Bayesian recruitment models, fitted (internal external) pilot date. can used design stage optimise pilot pre-specified decision rules guide progression, analysis stage produce probabilistic prediction time main trial recruits target.

title: Design and Analysis of Pilot Trials Assessing Recruitment Feasibility
text: Install released version fahb CRAN: can install development version fahb GitHub :
code: install.packages("fahb") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("DTWilson/fahb")

title: Design and Analysis of Pilot Trials Assessing Recruitment Feasibility
text: Suppose planning internal pilot trial aims recruit N = 320 participants m = 20 sites, expect recruitment complete three years. pilot analysis happen t = 0.5 years, class trial infeasible recruitment took longer rel_thr = 1.2 times expected recruitment time. variables N, m, t rel_thr define design variables. model parameters, purpose illustration use package defaults (see vignette associated paper discussion ). use fahb first encode design variables model parameters fahb_problem object, run simulations via forecast() finding possible decision rules operating characteristics lead :  Two types decision rules considered . Firstly, consider rules take form standard progression criteria suggested NIHR. table output, n_p minimum number participants recruited, m_p minimum number sites opened, r_p minimum rate recruitment (participants per site-year), progress main trial thresholds met. second type decision rules defined T_p, maximum expected time full recruitment conditional pilot data. progress main trial actual posterior predictive expectation lower threshold. Decision rules types characterised false positive (FPR) false negative (FNR) rates. estimated via simulation comparing decisions made underlying feasibility, determined threshold rel_thr. example, can attain FPR = 0.15 FNR = 0.34 proceed recruit least 10.4810.48 participants least 11 site, overall rate least 6.576.57 participants per site-year. can get slightly better operating characteristics use Bayesian progression rules instead. want improve pilot can increase design variable t_int internal pilot data.
code: library(fahb)  problem <- fahb_problem(N = 320, m = 20, t = 0.5, rel_thr = 1.2)  # Run the simulations problem <- forecast(problem)  # Find some candidate decision rules are their OCs design <- fahb_design(problem)  print(design) #> Standard progression criteria #>  #>     FPR        FNR         n_p          m_p        r_p #> 1   0.0 0.82817058 17.81878829 -0.059458005  9.6590446 #> 11  0.1 0.45825809  9.57670582  1.334793215  6.6483566 #> 21  0.2 0.31559939  6.90517099  0.182386148  6.0045943 #> 41  0.4 0.18030282  1.59558082  0.759740721  5.6010379 #> 51  0.5 0.12835116  2.70338476  1.696370342  4.5998899 #> 71  0.7 0.05625781  0.14306472  0.904175281  3.5567570 #> 81  0.8 0.03681067 -0.30186503  0.937890251  2.8416713 #> 91  0.9 0.02222531 -0.90403448 -0.022824150  1.9307604 #> 101 1.0 0.00000000  0.01221748  0.008838253 -0.7620012 #>  #> Bayesian approximation #>  #>     FPR        FNR      T_p #> 1   0.0 1.00000000 1.441843 #> 11  0.1 0.42366995 3.123216 #> 21  0.2 0.31309904 3.298833 #> 41  0.4 0.16335602 3.594781 #> 51  0.5 0.11543270 3.721615 #> 71  0.7 0.05723017 3.916746 #> 81  0.8 0.03653285 4.001302 #> 91  0.9 0.02139186 4.085859 #> 101 1.0 0.00000000 4.554172 #>  #> FPR - False Positive Rate #> FNR - False Negative Rate #>  #> n_p, m_p, r_p - Probabilistic thresholds for standard #>                 progression criteria on the number recruited, #>                 number of sites opened, and the recruitment rate #>                 (participants per site per year) respectively #>  #> T_p - Bayesian decision rule threshold for the posterior predictive #>       expected time until full recruitment plot(design)

what: Build a fahb analysis object — fahb_analysis
title: Build a fahb analysis object — fahb_analysis
text: Given fahb_problem object calculate summary statistics can inform progression decisions. include standard progression criteria statistics, expectation posterior predictive distribution time trial recruits.

title: Build a fahb analysis object — fahb_analysis
code: fahb_analysis(n_pilot, t_pilot, problem, site_t = NULL, bayes_model = NULL)

title: Build a fahb analysis object — fahb_analysis
text: n_pilot integer vector numbers recruited open site. t_pilot numeric vector time (years) site open. problem object class fahb_problem. site_t case external pilot, time taken pilot sites open. bayes_model optional object class brmsfit used Bayesian analysis via brms::update() avoid compiling new model.

title: Build a fahb analysis object — fahb_analysis
text: object class fahb_analysis.

title: Build a fahb analysis object — fahb_analysis
code: ## Example illustrating a full analysis workflow ## (Not run on CRAN due to Bayesian model fitting)  # \donttest{ problem <- fahb_problem() problem <- forecast(problem, n_sims = 500)  ## Pilot trial data n_pilot <- c(3, 5, 2) t_pilot <- c(0.5, 0.6, 0.4)  analysis <- fahb_analysis(   n_pilot = n_pilot,   t_pilot = t_pilot,   problem = problem ) #> Compiling the model... #> Error in .fun(model_code = .x1) :  #>   Boost not found; call install.packages('BH') #> Error in .fun(model_code = .x1): Boost not found; call install.packages('BH')  print(analysis) #> Error: object 'analysis' not found plot(analysis) #> Error: object 'analysis' not found # }

Build a fahb_problem object — fahb_problem
text: Given trial design set model hyperparameters, build object class fahb_problem.

title: Build a fahb_problem object — fahb_problem
code: fahb_problem(   N = 320,   m = 20,   t = 0.5,   n_ext = NULL,   m_ext = NULL,   rel_thr = 1.2,   so_hps = c(30, 2.85),   mean_rr_hps = c(2, 0.329),   sd_rr_hps = c(30, 100) )

title: Build a fahb_problem object — fahb_problem
text: N target sample size. m number recruiting sites. t timing pilot analysis years. n_ext number participants recruit external pilot. m_ext number sites open external pilot. rel_thr threshold discriminates feasible infeasible trials, multiple expected time recruit. so_hps site opening rate hyperparameters (shape rate Gamma prior). mean_rr_hps mean site recruitment rate hyperparameters (mean sd lognormal prior). sd_rr_hps variance site recruitment rates hyperparameters (shape rate Gamma prior).
